message.content:
Try HARD to REFUTE this plan through ONE lens. Default refuted=true if uncertain. Check /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board and /home/polaris/wt/flywheel.

DIAGNOSIS: FOUR MEASURED CAUSES + TWO STRATEGIC ERRORS. Measured: (1) ATTRIBUTION INVISIBLE — all 240 [n] markers and the reference list are deleted by the RACE cleaner before judging (cleaner_survival_test.py: 0/12 survival), so the judge reads naked assertions on a task whose instruction is 'only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles'; (2) CROSS-SOURCE INFERENCE BANNED BY CONSTRUCTION — entailment_judge.py NEUTRAL clause ('introduces a fact, entity, mechanism, or specificity NOT present in the SPAN', /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/llm/entailment_judge.py) plus synthesis_contract.py:197-205 delete exactly the sentence class that wins INSIGHT (weight 0.32, our worst dim 0.4238); (3) PHYSICALLY UNREADABLE — 12 paragraphs, median 677 words, 0 H3 (verified vs rank10_sections_compose/report.md); readability 0.3774 is worst on the board and bleeds into the single-call judging of all other dims; (4) CORPUS MIS-AIMED — 997 rows/919 URLs but only ~17-22 on-topic journal articles, 5 rows with authors, grey-lit top domains; the PG_AGENTIC loop was never pointed at this task. Strategic errors in the brief that this plan corrects: (A) UNITS — 0.5578 is cellcog's 100-task corpus-wide score; cellcog has NO task-72 raw_results; regression estimate of its task-72 is ~0.556±0.019, and the highest MEASURED task-72 score is bodhi's 0.5441. (B) INVERSION — the brief frames cellcog's un-sourced synthesis as a weakness to out-verify; it is the engineered strength behind insight 0.5708 at weight 0.32 (a dedicated 'Contested Debates and Novel Insights' section of 8 named, epistemically tagged syntheses 'no single paper articulates'). The winning play is: copy cellcog's insight engine and monograph architecture, keep our deterministic no-fabrication floor underneath it, and out-gun it on evidence volume/recency/method-texture where the rubric verbatim pays for 'Data and Factual Support' and 'Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized'.
RETRIEVAL: JOURNAL-FIRST AGENTIC LOOP + CITATION-GRAPH SNOWBALL, STOPPED BY A COVERAGE MATRIX, NOT A COUNT. (a) QUERY-GEN: run the existing planner perspectives (/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/agents/planner.py:309-531, PG_AGENTIC_SEED_QUERIES) but seed perspectives FROM the coverage matrix below, one per cell-group; queries phrased as scholarly-literature queries ('meta-analysis robot adoption employment', 'algorithmic management working conditions journal'), not news queries. (b) SEARCH: enable the idle loop — PG_AGENTIC_SEARCH_ENABLED=1 (/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/state.py:219), PG_AGENTIC_MAX_ROUNDS=15 (state.py:220, default 12), PG_AGENTIC_MAX_QUERIES=160 (state.py:221); INVERT the channel mix: PG_AGENTIC_ACADEMIC_PER_ROUND 2→8 and PG_AGENTIC_WEB_PER_ROUND 6→2 (state.py:229-230) so OpenAlex/Semantic-Scholar-class results dominate rounds (loop entry searcher.py:291). (c) EXPANSION — the decisive stage keyword search provably cannot do (Autor-Levy-Murnane's title contains neither 'AI' nor 'labor market'): after every 3 rounds, feed all newly found DOIs into /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/journal_corpus_build.py citation-graph expansion, BOTH directions (references + cited-by via Crossref/OpenAlex), depth 2 from canonical anchors (Autor-Levy-Murnane 2003; Acemoglu-Restrepo 2020; Frey-Osborne; Brynjolfsson-Mitchell-Rock; the 2024-25 meta-analyses). (d) FETCH: journal_corpus_fetch.py + deep_fetch.py (Crossref abstracts + Unpaywall OA full text); require retrievable abstract-or-fulltext, else keep metadata-only for context citations. (e) SELECT: hard gate = has DOI + journal + AUTHOR NAMES (the old corpus had 5/997 with authors — automatic reject now) + English; LLM on-topic gate scoring title+abstract against the task text (kills ResNet and Cognitive Psychology 1974); working papers (NBER/SSRN) quarantined into a 'contextual-only' bin, never primary evidence — this reproduces cellcog's Webb move. (f) WEIGHT: quality = journal standing + citation count + recency bonus (2024-2026, cellcog's refs are 41/104 from 2024+) + method-texture availability (does the abstract state N, design, identification?). (g) DEDUP: DOI-canonical; fuzzy-title for DOI-less. (h) CONSOLIDATE into evidence cards (next layer). STOPPING CONDITION = COVERAGE MATRIX, not a number: rows = restructuring dimensions the task names (employment/displacement, wages, skills/task content, polarization, autonomy/surveillance/algorithmic management, labor share, gender, Global South/informality, institutions/policy) x industries (manufacturing, healthcare, finance, retail, transport/logistics, professional services, education, platform/gig) x eras (computerization, robots/narrow AI, generative AI) x levels (platform/firm/regional/national/informal-sector). STOP when every reachable cell holds >=2 on-topic journal works OR a declared literature gap (gaps get narrated as findings — 'the peer-reviewed evidence on X is thin because...', which cellcog does and which no faithfulness gate can cut), AND total >=120 on-topic journal works with >=35% dated 2024+, AND one full round adds <2 new on-topic works per 20 queries. Target 120-150 works vs cellcog's ~100 — evidence volume is the lane where we out-score rather than match.
SYNTHESIS: THE CORRECTED-CORRECTED LINE. The brief's fix ('a mechanism may be asserted if ANY source in the corpus states it AND we cite that source') is necessary but NOT sufficient — grounding proved it would delete cellcog's highest-value section (§5 opens: 'syntheses that no single paper in the peer-reviewed literature articulates'), and 71% of cellcog's sentences carry no citation at all. FOUR LEGAL SENTENCE CLASSES: (1) GROUNDED FACT — asserts what a cited card's span states; entailment as before. (2) COMPOSITE BRIDGE (bodhi's winning move) — finding from paper A + mechanism from paper B, BOTH cited; fix /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/synthesis_contract.py:197-205 so the mechanism check runs against the declared mechanisms of ANY cited card in the corpus, not only the two premises being compared. (3) ADJUDICATION — cited finding A + cited finding B + an uncited, HEDGED resolution that names the resolving dimension drawn from declared card fields (level/horizon/method): 'The parallel is not a contradiction but a demonstration: firm-level aggregates can show growth while exposed workers face losses.' Legal iff the named dimension differs between the premise cards — deterministically checkable. (4) LABELED ANALYTICAL SYNTHESIS (cellcog's insight engine, the class the brief's rule bans) — an un-sourced mechanism/claim is legal IFF all three hold: (i) HEDGED verb from an allowlist ('may', 'could', 'we propose as analytical synthesis', 'suggests'); (ii) EPISTEMICALLY TAGGED with the graded taxonomy ([Established Finding] / [Emerging Pattern] / [Conceptual Argument] / [Analytical Hypothesis / Our Synthesis]); (iii) accompanied by an EXPLICIT GAP DECLARATION verified against the gap index ('No peer-reviewed study has yet measured...' — and our corpus confirms none does). That is cellcog's fabrication-free mechanism verbatim: assert the original claim AND tell the judge it is original and untested. STILL DELETED, DETERMINISTICALLY, ALWAYS: any number not in a source span; any study/organisation/author that does not exist; any attribution to someone who did not say it; any UNHEDGED-UNTAGGED mechanism no card states; forecasts stated as fact (hedged scenario-discussion tagged [Analytical Hypothesis] is legal; lunon's '2030 verdict' style is not); universals. The entailment judge's NEUTRAL clause stops being a sentence-killer and becomes a ROUTER: NEUTRAL sentences route to contract classes 2-4 instead of deletion.
COMPOSER: EXTEND /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cellcog_composer.py TO THE MEASURED #1 ARCHITECTURE — cellcog's actual moves, not the brief's misreadings. (1) CITATION TEMPLATE — cellcog's real workhorse (73 instances), NOT the brief's 'Writing in the JEP in 2019' contortion: 'Author(s) (YYYY), in the *Journal Name*, [verb] that ...' — author name and journal name in prose, YEAR STAYS IN PARENS (160/177 of cellcog's year-parens are this narrative form and it wins; the brief's 'year MUST be prose' rule is refuted). Zero [n] in body; references section retained for provenance (cleaner deletes it; costs nothing). (2) SPINE = THREE TECHNOLOGY ERAS (computerization/polarization → robots/narrow AI → generative AI) with sectors NESTED inside each era — cellcog's actual skeleton. 4IR handled by critique-and-subordinate: adopt as periodization in §2, contest it with journal-published critiques, decline the frame uncritically; NO four-revolutions comparison spine (that is WhaleCloud's move, 0.5396). (3) STRUCTURE: ~10 H2 + ~30 H3 + 6-8 H4; median paragraph ~100w, max <280w; body 12,500-14,000 words (cellcog 13,486; any 8k cap fights #1 with half its ammunition). (4) STRUCTURED ABSTRACT Objective/Methods/Findings/Contributions with a THESIS stated up front — our analogue of cellcog's 'Heterogeneity, not displacement, is the central peer-reviewed finding', derived from our contradiction index; Contributions line enumerates the syntheses by count. (5) SCOPE/METHODS/SOURCE-SELECTION section that ARGUES compliance: narrates journal-only criteria, analyzes the constraint as a publication bias with a stated compensation strategy, pre-empts the working-paper objection — plus point-of-use re-enactment whenever a contextual-only card appears ('Because X remains a working paper, we reference it contextually rather than as primary evidence'). (6) THE INSIGHT ENGINE: a dedicated 'Contested Debates and Novel Insights' H2 with 6-8 NAMED, BRANDED syntheses ('the informality buffer', 'the gender exposure reversal' style), each opened with first-person ownership, epistemically tagged via the graded taxonomy, hedged, and gap-declared — a numbered inventory of insights handed to the INSIGHT judge (weight 0.32). Include the legend paragraph: 'The convention is not cosmetic...'. (7) ONE MASTER KEY threaded through everything — level-of-analysis/institutional mediation at five named levels — so the document has a thesis, not coverage. (8) MONOGRAPH WIRING: ~30 internal cross-references ('a tension taken up in Section 5.3') — zero board systems except cellcog do this; cheap to generate in a second pass. (9) EPIGRAMMATIC PARAGRAPH CLOSES: unit of composition = evidence → evidence → one-line citation-free abstraction. (10) OPENING/CLOSING RING: open by staking a concrete evidentiary tension as 'the organizing puzzle', close by resolving it verbatim; final sentence refuses to forecast and positions the review above both sides of the public debate. NO meta-commentary opening ('This report synthesizes the retrieved research evidence...' is deleted from every template). (11) SECTOR TABLE: 8 sectors x dominant technology generation x primary finding x anchor studies — the instruction-coverage matrix rendered as content; thin cells narrated as gap-findings. (12) METHODS TEXTURE mandatory on cited empirical claims: N, design, identification, in prose from card fields.
EXPECTED: Honest sum from 0.4382, per-dimension, weighted (insight .32/compreh .29/instr .25/readability .14): READABILITY 0.3774 → ~0.520 (the board's observed ceiling — every non-POLARIS system sits 0.51-0.52; the fix is fully mechanical: 100w paragraphs, H3/H4, no walls) = +0.020 weighted. INSIGHT 0.4238 → ~0.555 (the four-class synthesis lane + the dedicated insight section + adjudication density + thesis; targets cellcog's own mechanism at its own dimension, cellcog=0.5708) = +0.042 weighted. COMPREHENSIVENESS 0.4549 → ~0.555 (120-150 on-topic journal works vs cellcog ~100, 13k body words, methods texture on every cited claim, sector matrix, recency through 2026 — the rubric's 'Data and Factual Support' and 'Depth and Representativeness' lanes, our genuine out-score lane) = +0.029 weighted. INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING 0.4409 → ~0.552 (journal-only compliance argued in prose and at point of use, working-paper quarantine, sectors covered visibly, 4IR engaged-and-critiqued; cellcog=0.5530) = +0.028 weighted. TOTAL ≈ 0.4382 + 0.119 ≈ 0.557. VERDICT: this LANDS ON 0.5578, it does not safely BEAT it — the central estimate beats bodhi's measured 0.5441 by ~4x the resolvable effect, but beating 0.5578 by a resolvable margin (mean >= 0.567) depends on the wheel converting the comprehensiveness-and-verification edge (more sources, newer sources, visible methods texture cellcog cannot exceed without our corpus) into +0.01 above the first-pass estimate. That is what iterations 3+ exist for, and the honest statement is: first full pass ~0.545-0.557; beat-with-margin requires 2-4 wheel iterations and is plausible, not guaranteed.
CEILING: Three honesty items. (1) THE TARGET ITSELF IS SOFT: 0.5578 is cellcog's corpus-wide 100-task average; cellcog published no task-72 score. Regression across the 9 dual-scored systems puts its task-72 at ~0.556 with residual SD 0.0094 — the true target is ~0.556±0.019, possibly below bodhi's measured 0.5441, possibly ~0.575. We therefore run two thresholds: beat-best-measured (0.5441+margin, decidable) and beat-estimate (0.5578+margin, the stretch). (2) READABILITY IS CAPPED: nobody on the board exceeds ~0.52; the judge compresses this dim; budget no gains above it. (3) WHAT COULD KEEP US UNDER 0.5578: (a) the insight dimension may pay cellcog's 8 syntheses partly for genuine domain-expert quality, not just the labeled-hedged-gap FORM — if our class-4 sentences are formulaic, the judge's prose will say so and the ceiling is ~0.55; (b) retrieval may saturate below ~120 on-topic works if OA full-text coverage of 2024-2026 journals is poor — then comprehensiveness caps near parity with cellcog instead of above it; (c) cross-dimension bleed works both ways — a 13.5k-word report with any residual wall-of-text regressions taxes everything. If after the kill rule fires the plateau is 0.545-0.555, the honest report is: we beat every measured task-72 score on the board, and whether we beat cellcog is unmeasurable at judge noise — exactly the sentence the operator should get, not a proxy.

LENS:
JUDGE-INVISIBILITY: RACE deletes all [n] markers and the bibliography, then scores us side-by-side against the 0.5102 reference in ONE call. Would the judge SEE each lever, and would it move a criterion into the top band?

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll investigate the judge-invisibility lens against this plan.

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""CLEANER-SURVIVAL TEST — which citation format survives RACE's LLM cleaner?

THE STAKES: RACE runs an LLM "ArticleCleaner" over every report BEFORE the judge sees it, instructed to
"remove all citation links, citation marks (such as [1], [2] ...), reference lists, footnotes".
VERIFIED on our own artifact: 345 [n] markers -> 0, and our entire 105-entry bibliography -> deleted.

So the judge currently sees POLARIS as a report with NO SOURCES AT ALL, on a task whose instruction is
"Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles."

The #1 system (cellcog) instead writes "Acemoglu and Restrepo (2019), in the Journal of Economic
Perspectives, show..." — ordinary prose. We BELIEVE the cleaner cannot remove that. But GPT-5.6 Sol
made the correct objection: **the cleaner is an LLM, not a regex.** Its prompt says "or other complex
citation formats". Whether author-year attribution survives is an EMPIRICAL question, and the entire
attribution lever rests on the answer.

This runs the PRODUCTION cleaner over the same 12 factual sentences rendered in 5 competing formats,
N times each, and reports what survives. No guessing.

Usage:
  set -a && . ./.env && set +a
  python scripts/cleaner_survival_test.py --runs 3
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import re
import sys
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
DRB = ROOT / 'third_party' / 'deep_research_bench'
sys.path.insert(0, str(DRB))

# 12 real facts from our own report, rendered 5 ways. Author/venue/year are REAL (Crossref-verified).
FACTS = [
    ('Automation generates a displacement effect that reduces labor\'s share of value added, countered by a reinstatement effect as new tasks reinstate labor',
     'Acemoglu and Restrepo', 2019, 'Journal of Economic Perspectives'),
    ('About 47 percent of total US employment is at risk of computerisation',
     'Frey and Osborne', 2017, 'Technological Forecasting and Social Change'),
    ('Generative AI raised the productivity of professional writing tasks by 40 percent and quality by 18 percent',
     'Noy and Zhang', 2023, 'Science'),
    ('Around 80 percent of the US workforce could have at least 10 percent of their tasks affected by large language models',
     'Eloundou et al.', 2024, 'Science'),
    ('The occupational AI exposure measure links AI advances to the abilities required by each occupation',
     'Felten et al.', 2021, 'Strategic Management Journal'),
    ('Employment polarization increased as routine task-intensive middle-wage jobs declined',
     'Autor et al.', 2013, 'American Economic Review'),
    ('Task content of occupations is the correct unit of analysis for technological displacement rather than skill level',
     'Autor', 2015, 'Journal of Economic Perspectives'),
    ('AI adoption in service firms reshapes the division of labor between employees and customers',
     'Buhalis et al.', 2019, 'Journal of Service Management'),
    ('Human resource management is being restructured by algorithmic decision systems',
     'Chowdhury et al.', 2023, 'Human Resource Management Review'),
    ('Entrepreneurial venture creation is being reshaped by artificial intelligence in the Fourth Industrial Revolution',
     'Chalmers et al.', 2021, 'Entrepreneurship Theory and Practice'),
    ('Wage growth for high-skilled workers diverged from that of routine workers over the period studied',
     'Baum-Snow and Pavan', 2013, 'The Review of Economics and Statistics'),
    ('The demand for social and analytical skills has risen relative to routine cognitive skills',
     'Autor et al.', 2006, 'American Economic Review'),
]


def render(fmt: str) -> str:
    """Render the 12 facts in one citation format, as a plausible report body."""
    out = ['## Empirical Evidence on AI and Labor Restructuring', '']
    for i, (fact, who, yr, venue) in enumerate(FACTS, 1):
        if fmt == 'A_numbered_marker':          # what POLARIS does today
            s = f'{fact}.[{i}]'
        elif fmt == 'B_parenthetical':          # (Author, Year)
            s = f'{fact} ({who}, {yr}).'
        elif fmt == 'C_narrative_author_year':  # cellcog's style: grammatical subject
            s = f'{who} ({yr}) show that {fact.lower()}.'
        elif fmt == 'D_narrative_with_journal':  # + the journal named in the sentence
            s = f'Writing in the {venue}, {who} ({yr}) show that {fact.lower()}.'
        elif fmt == 'E_journal_prefix':         # journal-first framing
            s = f'In their {yr} {venue} article, {who} show that {fact.lower()}.'
        else:
            raise ValueError(fmt)
        out.append(s)
        out.append('')
    if fmt == 'A_numbered_marker':
        out += ['## References', '']
        for i, (_, who, yr, venue) in enumerate(FACTS, 1):
            out.append(f'[{i}] {who} ({yr}). {venue}.')
    return '\n'.join(out)


def survives(cleaned: str, fmt: str) -> dict:
    """What survived, per fact?"""
    authors = sum(1 for (_, who, _, _) in FACTS if who.split()[0] in cleaned)
    years = sum(1 for (_, _, yr, _) in FACTS if str(yr) in cleaned)
    venues = sum(1 for (_, _, _, v) in FACTS if v.split()[0] in cleaned and v.split()[-1] in cleaned)
    facts = sum(1 for (f, _, _, _) in FACTS if f.split()[3] in cleaned)  # a content word from each fact
    markers = len(re.findall(r'\[\d+\]', cleaned))
    return {'authors': authors, 'years': years, 'venues': venues,
            'facts_intact': facts, 'markers_left': markers, 'words': len(cleaned.split())}


def main() -> int:
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument('--runs', type=int, default=3)
    a = ap.parse_args()

    from utils.clean_article import ArticleCleaner  # noqa: E402
    from utils.api import AIClient                  # noqa: E402

    client = AIClient()
    cleaner = ArticleCleaner(client)

    formats = ['A_numbered_marker', 'B_parenthetical', 'C_narrative_author_year',
               'D_narrative_with_journal', 'E_journal_prefix']

    print(f"=== CLEANER-SURVIVAL TEST — {len(FACTS)} facts x {len(formats)} formats x {a.runs} runs ===")
    print("    (the cleaner is an LLM, so this is measured, not assumed)\n")
    print(f"{'format':<26} {'authors':>8} {'years':>6} {'venues':>7} {'facts':>6} {'[n]':>4}   (of 12)")
    print('-' * 74)

    results = {}
    for fmt in formats:
        raw = render(fmt)
        agg = []
        for r in range(a.runs):
            try:
                cleaned = cleaner.chunk_clean_article(raw, language='en')
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"  {fmt}: cleaner error {e}")
                continue
            agg.append(survives(cleaned, fmt))
            if r == 0:
                Path(f'outputs/cleaner_test_{fmt}.txt').write_text(cleaned)
        if not agg:
            continue
        m = {k: sum(d[k] for d in agg) / len(agg) for k in agg[0]}
        results[fmt] = m
        print(f"{fmt:<26} {m['authors']:>8.1f} {m['years']:>6.1f} {m['venues']:>7.1f} "
              f"{m['facts_intact']:>6.1f} {m['markers_left']:>4.0f}")

    print('\n=== VERDICT ===')
    best = max(results, key=lambda f: (results[f]['authors'] + results[f]['venues'])) if results else None
    for fmt, m in results.items():
        keeps_sourcing = m['authors'] >= 10 and m['facts_intact'] >= 10
        verdict = 'SURVIVES — sourcing visible to the judge' if keeps_sourcing else 'SOURCING DESTROYED'
        print(f"  {fmt:<26} {verdict}")
    if best:
        print(f"\n  ** USE: {best} **")
        print(f"     authors surviving: {results[best]['authors']:.1f}/12, "
              f"venues: {results[best]['venues']:.1f}/12")
    Path('outputs/cleaner_survival.json').write_text(json.dumps(results, indent=1))
    print('\nwrote outputs/cleaner_survival.json + outputs/cleaner_test_<fmt>.txt')
    return 0


if __name__ == '__main__':
    raise SystemExit(main())

=== CLEAN ===
import json
import os
import time
import threading
import concurrent.futures
from tqdm import tqdm
from prompt.clean_prompt import clean_article_prompt_zh, clean_article_prompt_en
import logging

# Configure logging
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format='%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s')
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# Shared LLM-call concurrency cap. All cleaning calls (any chunk of any article)
# acquire from this semaphore so total inflight LLM requests ≤ LLM_CONCURRENCY.
# Override via env LLM_CONCURRENCY=N. Default 20.
_LLM_CONCURRENCY = int(os.environ.get("LLM_CONCURRENCY", "20"))
_llm_semaphore = threading.Semaphore(_LLM_CONCURRENCY)
logger.info(f"LLM call concurrency cap = {_LLM_CONCURRENCY}")


class ArticleCleaner:
    def __init__(self, clean_agent):
        self.clean_agent = clean_agent
        self.min_valid_length = 100
    
    def _get_clean_prompt(self, language="zh"):
        """Return language-specific cleaning prompt template"""
        return clean_article_prompt_zh if language == "zh" else clean_article_prompt_en
    
    def _is_valid_result(self, text):
        """Check if cleaning result is valid"""
        return text and len(text.strip()) >= self.min_valid_length
    
    def _update_progress(self, pbar, pbar_lock):
        """Update progress bar safely"""
        if pbar and pbar_lock:
            with pbar_lock:
                pbar.update(1)
    
    def _is_token_limit_error(self, error):
        """Check if error is related to token limit"""
        error_str = str(error).lower()
        return "tokens" in error_str and "less than" in error_str
    
    _TRUNCATED = "__TRUNCATED__"

    def _clean_text(self, text, language="zh", max_retries=None):
        """Try to clean text. Returns string (incl. "") on success,
        self._TRUNCATED on output overflow, or None after all retries.
        max_retries kept for signature compatibility but the canonical budget
        is _RETRY_BACKOFFS_S (3 attempts with exponential backoff).
        """
        clean_prompt = self._get_clean_prompt(language)
        user_prompt = clean_prompt.format(article=text)
        backoffs = self._RETRY_BACKOFFS_S

        last_err = None
        for attempt, sleep_s in enumerate([0, *backoffs[:-1]]):
            if sleep_s:
                time.sleep(sleep_s)
            try:
                with _llm_semaphore:
                    result, stop_reason = self.clean_agent.generate(
                        user_prompt=user_prompt, system_prompt="",
                        return_metadata=True, stage="clean",
                    )
                if stop_reason in ("max_tokens", "length"):
                    logger.warning(f"Output truncated (stop_reason={stop_reason}), needs chunking")
                    return self._TRUNCATED
                # Accept any string (including "") — empty means the chunk
                # was entirely a reference list, per prompt contract.
                if result is not None:
                    return result
                logger.warning(f"Got None from API, attempt {attempt+1}/{len(backoffs)}")
            except Exception as e:
                last_err = e
                logger.error(f"API call error (attempt {attempt+1}/{len(backoffs)}): {e}")
                if self._is_token_limit_error(e):
                    logger.info("Article too long, needs chunking")

# 清洗文章的提示词，去除引用格式、参考文献等内容
clean_article_prompt_zh = """
<system_role>你是一名专业的文章编辑，擅长整理和清洗文章内容。</system_role>

<user_prompt>
下面给你的内容可能是一篇完整研究文章，也可能只是其中的一个连续片段（chunk）。
请只针对输入的这部分内容进行清洗，不要假设、补全或引用片段之外的上下文。

清洗目标：去除所有引用链接、引用标记（如[1]、[2]、1、2 等或其他复杂引用格式）、参考文献列表、脚注，并确保文章内容连贯流畅。
保留文章的所有其他原本内容、只移除引用。如果文章中使用引用标记中的内容作为语句的一部分，保留这其中的文字内容，移除其他标记。

特殊情况：如果输入的这段内容整段都属于某个文章的参考文献区（reference 列表 / 脚注定义等），不包含任何正文，请返回空字符串。

输入内容：
"{article}"

请返回清洗后的全文，不要添加任何额外说明或评论。
</user_prompt>
"""

clean_article_prompt_en = """
<system_role>You are a professional article editor who is good at cleaning and refining article content.</system_role>

<user_prompt>
The input below may be a complete research article or just a continuous fragment (chunk) of one.
Operate only on the content provided; do not assume, complete, or quote any context outside this fragment.

Cleaning goal: remove all citation links, citation marks (such as [1], [2], 1, 2, etc. or other complex citation formats), reference lists, footnotes, and ensure the content reads smoothly.
Keep all other original content; remove only citations. If a citation mark wraps content that is part of a sentence, keep the text inside and drop only the marks.

Special case: if the entire input is a reference / bibliography / footnote-definition section of some article with no body text, return an empty string.

Input:
"{article}"

Return the cleaned text in full, without any additional explanation or comment.
</user_prompt>
"""

content.text:
I ran the production RACE cleaner on cellcog's actual task-72 §5 text. The lens produces a decisive, measured refutation of one load-bearing composer decision.

**What I measured** (`/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/third_party/deep_research_bench/prompt/clean_prompt.py` cleaner, run twice over a 988-word chunk of cellcog-max id=72 containing the legend + tagged syntheses + 14 narrative citations):

| feature | raw | cleaned run 0 | cleaned run 1 |
|---|---|---|---|
| epistemic tags `*[Established Finding]*` etc. | 7 | 7 | 7 |
| H2/H3 headings | 5 | 5 | 5 |
| **parenthetical years `(2019)`** | **14** | **0** | **0** |

And the pre-existing `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/cleaner_survival.json` (12 facts × 5 formats):
- `C_narrative_author_year` ("Acemoglu and Restrepo (2019) show…"): authors **5.0/12**, years **0.0/12**
- `D_narrative_with_journal` ("Writing in the *JEP*, Acemoglu and Restrepo (2019) show…"): authors 10/12, venues 12/12, years **0.0/12**
- `E_journal_prefix` ("In their 2019 *JEP* article, Acemoglu and Restrepo show…"): authors 10/12, venues 9/12, years **10/12** — the only format where the year reaches the judge

**The refutation.** COMPOSER item (1) mandates "YEAR STAYS IN PARENS (160/177 of cellcog's year-parens are this narrative form and it wins; the brief's 'year MUST be prose' rule is refuted)." That count was taken from cellcog's **pre-cleaner** submission. The judge never saw a single one of those 177 year-parens — the cleaner deletes 100% of them, verified on cellcog's own sentences. The plan reproduces exactly the category error it convicts the brief of in DIAGNOSIS (1): inferring judge-visible strength from an artifact the judge doesn't read. It then uses that non-evidence to overturn the one rule the project's only measurement supports, and dismisses format E — the strictly dominant format — as a "contortion."

**Why it costs score, not just tidiness.** Recency is a named pillar of the plan's two biggest bets: COMPREHENSIVENESS +0.029 ("recency through 2026", ">=35% dated 2024+", retrieval recency bonus) and the beat-with-margin condition ("newer sources … cellcog cannot exceed"). Under the mandated template the judge sees **zero years anywhere in the body** — a 2026 meta-analysis and a 1974 paper are indistinguishable. The retrieval layer's recency weighting is real work that terminates before the judge.

**Second, smaller lens hit, same root.** "120-150 works vs cellcog's ~100" converts to judge-visible signal only through prose attributions (`[n]` and the reference list are deleted; the plan concedes this). The plan sets a *corpus* target but no *prose-attribution* target. Cellcog gets ~73 narrated works into the judge's view inside **16,334 words** (measured — not the 13,486 the plan states); the plan caps the body at 12.5–14k. A larger corpus rendered at lower prose-attribution density registers as ≤ parity on the exact dimension chosen as the out-score lane.

**What the lens confirms as safe:** the insight engine survives intact — epistemic tags in square brackets are *not* eaten as "citation marks" (7/7, both runs), and H2/H3 structure survives, so the 0.32-weight insight lever and the readability lever do reach the judge.

**Fix (cheap, mechanical):** switch the composer's citation template to the measured-dominant form — year and journal in prose, author as subject: `In their 2024 *Journal of Economic Perspectives* article, Acemoglu and Restrepo show that …` (E: authors 10/12, years 10/12, venues 9/12). Add a hard composer target of ≥110 distinct prose attributions with ≥35% carrying a prose year in 2024–2026, and raise the body target to ~15–16k to match cellcog's real length. Then re-run `scripts/cleaner_survival_test.py` on the actual composed draft, not on synthetic sentences, before trusting any per-dimension estimate.

input.reasoning:
REFUTED on the judge-invisibility lens by direct measurement against the production RACE cleaner.

I ran the production ArticleCleaner (/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/third_party/deep_research_bench/prompt/clean_prompt.py) twice over a 988-word chunk of cellcog-max id=72 (/home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/cellcog-max.jsonl) containing the legend paragraph, the tagged syntheses, and 14 narrative citations.

MEASURED (raw -> cleaned run0 / run1):
- epistemic tags (*[Established Finding]* etc.): 7 -> 7 / 7  (SURVIVE)
- H2/H3 headings: 5 -> 5 / 5  (SURVIVE)
- parenthetical years "(2019)": 14 -> 0 / 0  (DESTROYED, 100%)

Corroborated by the project's existing /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/cleaner_survival.json (12 facts x 5 formats):
- C_narrative_author_year "Acemoglu and Restrepo (2019) show...": authors 5.0/12, years 0.0/12
- D_narrative_with_journal "Writing in the JEP, Acemoglu and Restrepo (2019) show...": authors 10/12, venues 12/12, years 0.0/12
- E_journal_prefix "In their 2019 JEP article, Acemoglu and Restrepo show...": authors 10/12, venues 9/12, years 10/12  <- the ONLY format where the year reaches the judge

THE REFUTATION. COMPOSER item (1) explicitly mandates "YEAR STAYS IN PARENS (160/177 of cellcog's year-parens are this narrative form and it wins; the brief's 'year MUST be prose' rule is refuted)." That 160/177 count was taken from cellcog's PRE-CLEANER submission. The judge never saw one of those year-parens: the cleaner deletes 100% of them, verified on cellcog's own sentences. The plan therefore commits precisely the category error it convicts the brief of in DIAGNOSIS (1) -- inferring judge-visible strength from an artifact the judge does not read -- and uses that non-evidence to overturn the one rule the project's only measurement actually supports, dismissing format E (the strictly dominant format on every axis) as a "contortion."

WHY IT COSTS SCORE, NOT JUST TIDINESS. Recency is a named pillar of the plan's two biggest bets: COMPREHENSIVENESS +0.029 ("recency through 2026", ">=35% dated 2024+", retrieval recency bonus, "newer sources ... cellcog cannot exceed") and the stated beat-with-margin condition (converting the comprehensiveness edge into +0.01). Under the mandated template the judge sees ZERO years anywhere in the body -- a 2026 meta-analysis and a 1974 paper are indistinguishable to it. The entire retrieval-side recency weighting is real work that terminates before the judge. The lever is invisible, so it cannot move a criterion into the top band.

SECOND LENS HIT (same root). "120-150 works vs cellcog's ~100" converts to judge-visible signal only via prose attributions, since [n] markers and the reference list are deleted (the plan concedes this). The plan sets a CORPUS target but no PROSE-ATTRIBUTION target. Cellcog gets ~73 narrated works in front of the judge inside 16,334 words (measured; the plan states 13,486 -- also wrong), while the plan caps the body at 12,500-14,000 words. A bigger corpus rendered at lower prose-attribution density reads to the judge as parity-or-worse on the exact dimension the plan chose as its out-score lane.

WHAT THE LENS CONFIRMS AS SAFE (so this is a targeted refutation, not a wholesale one): the 0.32-weight insight engine survives -- square-bracket epistemic tags are NOT eaten as "citation marks" (7/7, both runs) -- and headings survive, so the readability levers (H3/H4, short paragraphs) do reach the judge. Author names and journal names in prose survive at 10/12 and 12/12. The plan's core architecture is sound; its citation template and its evidence-volume-to-judge conversion are not.
input.fix:
1) Replace the COMPOSER citation template with the measured-dominant form E (year and journal in PROSE, author as grammatical subject): "In their 2024 *Journal of Economic Perspectives* article, Acemoglu and Restrepo show that ..." -- measured authors 10/12, years 10/12, venues 9/12 post-cleaner, versus 0/12 years for every parenthetical variant. Delete the "YEAR STAYS IN PARENS" rule and the 160/177 justification (it counts features of an un-cleaned artifact).
2) Add a hard composer target on judge-visible attribution, not corpus size: >=110 distinct in-prose author+journal attributions, of which >=35% carry a prose year in 2024-2026. Corpus count (120-150) is an input; prose-attribution count is the only thing the judge can score.
3) Raise the body target to ~15,000-16,000 words to match cellcog's real length (measured 16,334, not the 13,486 stated in the plan), otherwise the plan fights the #1 with less ammunition on its own architecture.
4) Keep the epistemic tags exactly as specified -- verified to survive the cleaner 7/7 -- and keep the H2/H3/H4 plan; those levers are confirmed judge-visible.
5) Before trusting any per-dimension estimate, re-run /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cleaner_survival_test.py against the ACTUAL composed draft (not synthetic sentences) and assert post-cleaner: 0 lost tags, >=100 surviving author names, >=35 surviving 2024+ years, all headings intact. Make that a gate, not a spot check.
